title: Condensed milk milk tart – no baking needed

seo.title: Condensed milk milk tart - no baking needed - Marinda Kook

html:
My sweet tooth tempts me now and then to whip something up together. This milk tart recipe has been doing the rounds in my family for a long time. What's surprising is that it isn't too sweet – even though it's made with condensed milk. Keep it in the fridge for at least 4 hours before you cut it. So delicious!!!!!!!

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 1 tin condensed milk
  • 700 ml boiling water
  • 20 ml butter
  • 50 ml Maizena
  • 60 ml cold water
  • 2 eggs – separated
  • 10 ml vanilla extract
  • pinch of salt
  • now you can finely grate the zest of one orange or lemon
  • 1 packet tennis biscuits
  • cinnamon sugar ( mix your own: 100 ml sugar mixed with 10 ml ground cinnamon)

NOW:

  1. pack the tennis biscuits in the bottom of a dish sprayed with Spray and Cook
  2. put the condensed milk, boiling water, vanilla, citrus zest and butter in a pot and bring it to the boil
  3. meanwhile mix the salt and Maizena with the cold water to a paste and whisk in the egg yolks
  4. whisk the egg whites in a separate clean bowl to soft peak stage
  5. take the pot off the stove and quickly stir in the Maizena/egg mixture with a wire whisk
  6. put it back on the plate and keep whisking until it thickens – it happens quickly
  7. NOW: scoop a spoonful of the mixture into the egg white and stir in
  8. scoop in another spoonful into the egg white
  9. gently fold half of that mixture back into the mixture in your pot
  10. stir in the rest
  11. spoon it over your tennis biscuits and sprinkle generously with cinnamon sugar
  12. put it in the fridge for at least four hours

I give you my word that you won't be disappointed. It's soft as velvet. It's as if a little cloud of love comes to rest on your tongue.
